water.name: Kirch Wildlife Management Area: All other waters

sources.title: State of Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners — Fishing Seasons CR 25-16 (Jan 1, 2026 – Dec 31, 2027)
sources.quotedText: Kirch Wildlife Management Area: All other waters within the Kirch Wildlife Management Area. Season: Open year around except the upper portions of Adams-McGill, Cold Springs and Haymeadow reservoirs closed to trespass February 15 through August 15 as posted. A 100-yard area as posted around the inflow from Hot Creek to Adams-McGill Reservoir is closed to fishing January 1 through April 1. Limits — Daily: 5 trout and 15 warmwater game fish of which not more than 10 may be black bass. No limit on bullheads (unlimited possession). Limit is 5 black bass from April 1 through June 30. Possession: 10 trout and 30 warmwater game fish of which 20 may be black bass. Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

sources.title: Adams-McGill Reservoir at Kirch WMA (Sunnyside) — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: A self-sustaining largemouth bass fishery exists in the reservoir as well as a put-and-take rainbow trout fishery. Adams-McGill Reservoir is located on the Kirch Wildlife Management Area (formerly Sunnyside) in the White River Valley of Nye County. Stocking Updates: 1955 Rainbow Trout, 8.6, 04-11-2026; 2002 Rainbow Trout, 9.3, 04-18-2024; 4092 Rainbow Trout, 8.6, 04-27-2023; 4238 Rainbow Trout, 8.7, 04-26-2023.

sources.title: Haymeadow Reservoir at Kirch WMA — Nevada Department of Wildlife
sources.quotedText: Haymeadow Reservoir was impounded in 1967 and is located on the Kirch Wildlife Management Area (KWMA), formerly Sunnyside. Its main purpose is to increase the amount of wetlands for waterfowl and to create a sport fishery. Stocking Updates: 4968 Rainbow Trout, 8.9, 05-05-2026; 4991 Rainbow Trout, 9.1, 05-03-2026; 5070 Rainbow trout, 8.6, 05-01-2026; 5010 Rainbow Trout, 9.5, 04-22-2025; 5015 Rainbow Trout, 9.5, 04-22-2025; 5568 Rainbow Trout, 9.0, 10-30-2024; 5024 Rainbow Trout, 9.3, 10-24-2024; 3900 Rainbow Trout, 9.2, 06-05-2024; 5016 Rainbow Trout, 9.8, 05-09-2024; 4862 Rainbow Trout, 8.7, 04-17-2023; 5138 Rainbow Trout, 9.7, 04-13-2023.

sources.title: Cold Springs Reservoir at Kirch WMA — Nevada Department of Wildlife
Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

parameters.note: Open year-round except the upper portions of Adams-McGill, Cold Springs and Haymeadow reservoirs are closed to trespass February 15 through August 15 as posted (waterfowl nesting protection).

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Nye County, Kirch Wildlife Management Area
regulations.humanSummary: All other waters within the Kirch Wildlife Management Area are open to fishing year-round, except the upper portions of Adams-McGill, Cold Springs, and Haymeadow reservoirs, which are closed to trespass February 15 through August 15 as posted.
regulations.verbatimText: Season: Open year around except the upper portions of Adams-McGill, Cold Springs and Haymeadow reservoirs closed to trespass February 15 through August 15 as posted.

parameters.note: The upper portions of Adams-McGill, Cold Springs and Haymeadow reservoirs are closed to trespass (not just fishing) from February 15 through August 15 as posted, to protect nesting waterfowl.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Nye County, Kirch Wildlife Management Area
regulations.humanSummary: The upper portions of Adams-McGill, Cold Springs, and Haymeadow reservoirs are closed to all trespass (including fishing access) February 15 through August 15, as posted.
regulations.verbatimText: Season: Open year around except the upper portions of Adams-McGill, Cold Springs and Haymeadow reservoirs closed to trespass February 15 through August 15 as posted.

parameters.note: A 100-yard area, as posted, around the inflow from Hot Creek to Adams-McGill Reservoir is closed to fishing January 1 through April 1.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Nye County, Kirch Wildlife Management Area
regulations.humanSummary: A 100-yard area (as posted) around the inflow of Hot Creek into Adams-McGill Reservoir is closed to fishing January 1 through April 1.
regulations.verbatimText: A 100-yard area as posted around the inflow from Hot Creek to Adams-McGill Reservoir is closed to fishing January 1 through April 1.

parameters.counting_rule: Daily limit is 5 trout plus 15 warmwater game fish, of which not more than 10 may be black bass (reduced to 5 black bass daily April 1 through June 30). No limit on bullheads (unlimited possession, not counted against the warmwater game fish limit). Possession limit is 10 trout plus 30 warmwater game fish, of which up to 20 may be black bass.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Nye County, Kirch Wildlife Management Area
regulations.humanSummary: Daily limit: 5 trout and 15 warmwater game fish (no more than 10 black bass, reduced to 5 black bass April 1–June 30); bullheads unlimited. Possession limit: 10 trout and 30 warmwater game fish (up to 20 black bass).
regulations.verbatimText: Limits — Daily: 5 trout and 15 warmwater game fish of which not more than 10 may be black bass. No limit on bullheads (unlimited possession). Limit is 5 black bass from April 1 through June 30. Possession: 10 trout and 30 warmwater game fish of which 20 may be black bass.

regulations.citation: NBWC CR 25-16 — Nye County, Kirch Wildlife Management Area
regulations.humanSummary: Fishing is allowed any hour of the day or night.
regulations.verbatimText: Hours: Any hour of the day or night.

parameters.note: Standard Nevada resident/nonresident fishing license required for anglers 12 and older.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; no license required under age 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
